# Launcher — Complete Documentation

> A Raycast-style ⌘K command launcher with hierarchical drill-down, fuzzy search, sidebar categories, recents, and favorites. Built on shadcn Command + Dialog primitives with Zustand state management.

## Table of Contents
1. Installation
2. Quick Start
3. Core Concepts
4. Component API
5. Store API
6. Type Definitions
7. Customization
8. Integration Patterns
9. Testing
10. Architecture

---

## 1. Installation

```bash
npx shadcn add https://launcher.tunajam.com/r/launcher.json
```

This will install:
- All launcher components
- Store factory (`createLauncherStore`)
- Search utilities
- Type definitions

### Dependencies

The launcher requires these packages:

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "zustand": "^5.0.0",
    "fuse.js": "^7.0.0",
    "@phosphor-icons/react": "^2.0.0"
  }
}
```

And these shadcn components:
- `command`
- `dialog`
- `badge`
- `separator`

---

## 2. Quick Start

```tsx
import { Launcher, createLauncherStore, type LauncherItem } from "@/registry/new-york/launcher/launcher";
import { Users, User, Settings } from "@phosphor-icons/react";

// 1. Define your data
const manifest: LauncherItem[] = [
  {
    id: "teams",
    name: "Teams",
    description: "Manage your teams",
    icon: Users,
    category: "Apps",
    tags: ["people", "teams"],
    children: [
      {
        id: "teams-my",
        name: "My Teams",
        description: "Your team memberships",
        icon: User,
        category: "Apps",
        tags: ["my", "teams"],
      },
    ],
  },
];

// 2. Create the store
const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({ manifest });

// 3. Add to your layout
export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <Launcher store={useLauncherStore} />
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

Press `⌘K` (or `Ctrl+K`) to open the launcher.

---

## 3. Core Concepts

### Hierarchical Navigation

The launcher supports unlimited nesting via the `children` property:

```tsx
{
  id: "settings",
  name: "Settings",
  icon: Settings,
  category: "System",
  tags: ["config"],
  children: [
    {
      id: "settings-profile",
      name: "Profile",
      icon: User,
      category: "System",
      tags: ["account"],
      children: [
        {
          id: "settings-profile-photo",
          name: "Change Photo",
          icon: Camera,
          category: "System",
          tags: ["avatar"],
          action: () => console.log("change photo"),
        },
      ],
    },
  ],
}
```

### Actions vs URLs vs Children

Items can have:
- **`children`**: Drill into a submenu
- **`action`**: Execute a function (closes launcher)
- **`url`**: Open external link in new tab (closes launcher)

Precedence: `children` > `action` = `url`

### Search

Search is powered by Fuse.js with weighted fields:

```ts
const defaultWeights = {
  name: 3.0,       // Exact matches prioritized
  aliases: 2.0,    // Alternative names
  keywords: 1.5,   // Related terms
  tags: 1.0,       // Categories/labels
  description: 0.5 // Full text
};
```

### Recents & Favorites

- **Recents**: Last 20 selected items (auto-populated)
- **Favorites**: User-starred items (persist to localStorage)

Both are accessible via special sidebar categories:
- `__recent`
- `__favorites`

---

## 4. Component API

### `<Launcher />`

Main component that orchestrates all subcomponents.

```tsx
interface LauncherProps {
  store: ReturnType<typeof createLauncherStore>;
  categories?: Category[];
  branding?: {
    icon?: React.ReactNode;
    label?: string;
  };
}
```

**Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `store` | Zustand Store | Required | Store from `createLauncherStore()` |
| `categories` | `Category[]` | `[All, Recent, Favorites]` | Sidebar category list |
| `branding` | `{ icon, label }` | `{ icon: Diamond, label: "launcher" }` | Header/footer branding |

**Example:**

```tsx
<Launcher 
  store={useLauncherStore} 
  categories={customCategories}
  branding={{ icon: <Rocket />, label: "my-app" }}
/>
```

### `<LauncherInput />`

Search input with breadcrumbs.

```tsx
interface LauncherInputProps {
  store: ReturnType<typeof createLauncherStore>;
  branding?: { icon?: React.ReactNode; label?: string; };
}
```

### `<LauncherSidebar />`

Category navigation sidebar.

```tsx
interface LauncherSidebarProps {
  store: ReturnType<typeof createLauncherStore>;
  categories?: Category[];
}
```

### `<LauncherResults />`

Scrollable results list with favorites.

```tsx
interface LauncherResultsProps {
  store: ReturnType<typeof createLauncherStore>;
}
```

### `<LauncherFooter />`

Keyboard shortcuts hint bar.

```tsx
interface LauncherFooterProps {
  store: ReturnType<typeof createLauncherStore>;
  branding?: { icon?: React.ReactNode; label?: string; };
}
```

---

## 5. Store API

### `createLauncherStore(config)`

Factory function that returns a Zustand store.

```ts
interface LauncherConfig {
  manifest: LauncherItem[];
  searchWeights?: SearchWeights;
  persistKey?: string;
}

const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore(config);
```

**Config:**

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `manifest` | `LauncherItem[]` | Required | Root-level items |
| `searchWeights` | `SearchWeights` | `defaultWeights` | Fuse.js field weights |
| `persistKey` | `string` | `"launcher-storage"` | localStorage key |

### State

```ts
{
  open: boolean;                    // Dialog open/closed
  query: string;                    // Search query
  selectedCategory: string | null;  // Active sidebar filter
  navigationStack: NavigationLevel[]; // Breadcrumb trail
  recents: string[];                // Recent item IDs
  favorites: string[];              // Favorited item IDs
}
```

### Actions

```ts
setOpen(open: boolean): void
setQuery(query: string): void
setSelectedCategory(category: string | null): void
selectItem(item: LauncherItem): void
pushNavigation(item: LauncherItem): void
popNavigation(): void
resetNavigation(): void
toggleFavorite(id: string): void
setNavigationStack(updater): void
getBreadcrumbs(): string[]
getDisplayResults(): LauncherItem[]
```

**Example:**

```tsx
const openLauncher = () => useLauncherStore.getState().setOpen(true);
const addToFavorites = (id: string) => useLauncherStore.getState().toggleFavorite(id);
```

---

## 6. Type Definitions

### `LauncherItem`

```ts
interface LauncherItem {
  id: string;                    // Unique identifier
  name: string;                  // Display name
  description?: string;          // Subtitle
  icon: PhosphorIcon;            // @phosphor-icons/react icon
  category: string;              // Group label
  tags: string[];                // Search tags
  aliases?: string[];            // Alternative names
  keywords?: string[];           // Related search terms
  prefixes?: string[];           // Command-style prefixes (e.g., "/edit")
  children?: LauncherItem[];     // Nested items
  action?: () => void;           // Callback (closes launcher)
  url?: string;                  // External link (opens in new tab)
}
```

### `Category`

```ts
interface Category {
  id: string;           // Must match LauncherItem.category
  name: string;         // Display label
  icon?: PhosphorIcon;  // Sidebar icon
  isDivider?: boolean;  // Render as separator
}
```

### `NavigationLevel`

```ts
interface NavigationLevel {
  item: LauncherItem | null;  // null = root level
  results: LauncherItem[];    // Visible items at this level
}
```

### `SearchWeights`

```ts
interface SearchWeights {
  name: number;
  aliases: number;
  keywords: number;
  tags: number;
  description: number;
}
```

---

## 7. Customization

### Custom Categories

```tsx
import { SquaresFour, Folder, Settings, Star, ClockCounterClockwise } from "@phosphor-icons/react";

const categories: Category[] = [
  { id: "all", name: "All", icon: SquaresFour },
  { id: "__recent", name: "Recent", icon: ClockCounterClockwise },
  { id: "__favorites", name: "Favorites", icon: Star },
  { isDivider: true },
  { id: "Files", name: "Files", icon: Folder },
  { id: "Settings", name: "Settings", icon: Settings },
];

<Launcher store={useLauncherStore} categories={categories} />
```

### Custom Search Weights

```tsx
const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({
  manifest,
  searchWeights: {
    name: 4.0,
    aliases: 3.0,
    keywords: 2.0,
    tags: 1.5,
    description: 0.3,
  },
});
```

### Custom Branding

```tsx
import { Rocket } from "@phosphor-icons/react";

const branding = {
  icon: <Rocket size={12} weight="fill" className="text-primary/40" />,
  label: "acme-launcher",
};

<Launcher store={useLauncherStore} branding={branding} />
```

### Styling

All components use shadcn tokens. Override via Tailwind classes or CSS variables:

```css
/* Scrollbar styling */
.launcher-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar {
  width: 6px;
}

.launcher-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
  background: hsl(var(--muted));
  border-radius: 3px;
}

/* Custom dialog positioning */
[role="dialog"][data-launcher] {
  top: 10% !important;
}
```

---

## 8. Integration Patterns

### Next.js App Router

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
import { Launcher, createLauncherStore } from "@/registry/new-york/launcher/launcher";
import { manifest } from "@/lib/launcher-manifest";

const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({ manifest });

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <body>
        {children}
        <Launcher store={useLauncherStore} />
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

### Dynamic Manifest (Server Actions)

```tsx
"use client";

import { Launcher, createLauncherStore } from "@/registry/new-york/launcher/launcher";
import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

export function DynamicLauncher() {
  const [manifest, setManifest] = useState([]);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    fetch("/api/launcher-data")
      .then(res => res.json())
      .then(setManifest);
  }, []);

  const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({ manifest });
  
  return <Launcher store={useLauncherStore} />;
}
```

### Programmatic Control

```tsx
"use client";

import { Launcher, createLauncherStore } from "@/registry/new-york/launcher/launcher";
import { useEffect } from "react";

const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({ manifest });

export function App() {
  useEffect(() => {
    // Open launcher on mount
    useLauncherStore.getState().setOpen(true);
    
    // Programmatically navigate
    const settingsItem = manifest.find(i => i.id === "settings");
    if (settingsItem) {
      useLauncherStore.getState().selectItem(settingsItem);
    }
  }, []);

  return <Launcher store={useLauncherStore} />;
}
```

---

## 9. Testing

### Vitest Example

```tsx
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { Launcher, createLauncherStore } from "@/registry/new-york/launcher/launcher";
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";

describe("Launcher", () => {
  it("renders and opens on ⌘K", async () => {
    const useLauncherStore = createLauncherStore({ manifest: [] });
    render(<Launcher store={useLauncherStore} />);
    
    // Trigger keyboard shortcut
    fireEvent.keyDown(document, { key: "k", metaKey: true });
    
    expect(screen.getByRole("dialog")).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

### Playwright Example

```ts
import { test, expect } from "@playwright/test";

test("launcher opens with ⌘K", async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto("/");
  
  await page.keyboard.press("Meta+K");
  
  await expect(page.locator("[role='dialog']")).toBeVisible();
});
```

**Note:** The store automatically disables localStorage persistence when `NODE_ENV === "test"` for deterministic testing.

---

## 10. Architecture

### Component Hierarchy

```
<Launcher>                    # Root container
  <CommandDialog>             # shadcn Dialog wrapper
    <Command>                 # shadcn Command (cmdk)
      <LauncherInput>         # Search + breadcrumbs
      <LauncherSidebar>       # Category filters
      <LauncherResults>       # Item list
      <LauncherFooter>        # Keyboard hints
```

### State Flow

```
User input (⌘K, search, click)
  → Zustand store action
  → State update
  → Component re-render (via Zustand selectors)
  → UI reflects new state
```

### Search Pipeline

```
query → searchItems()
  → Fuse.js search with weights
  → Sort by: exact match > starts-with > fuzzy score
  → Boost recents (+50) and favorites (+100)
  → Return sorted results
```

### Navigation Stack

```
Root (item: null)
  → Drill into "Settings" (item: settings, results: settings.children)
  → Drill into "Profile" (item: profile, results: profile.children)
```

Escape pops the stack. Breadcrumbs are clickable to jump to any level.

### Persistence

```
localStorage["launcher-storage"] = {
  recents: ["item-id-1", "item-id-2", ...],
  favorites: ["item-id-3", ...]
}
```

Recents cap at 20. Favorites have no limit.

---

## FAQ

**Q: Can I use Lucide icons instead of Phosphor?**  
A: Yes! Swap the icon type in `LauncherItem`:

```ts
import { Icon } from "lucide-react";

interface LauncherItem {
  icon: Icon; // Change from PhosphorIcon
  // ...
}
```

**Q: How do I add keyboard shortcuts to items?**  
A: Use the `prefixes` field:

```ts
{
  id: "new-file",
  name: "New File",
  prefixes: ["/new", "⌘N"],
  // ...
}
```

**Q: Can I disable favorites?**  
A: Remove `__favorites` from your categories array.

**Q: How do I add loading states?**  
A: Extend the store with a custom `loading` state:

```tsx
interface ExtendedLauncherState extends LauncherState {
  loading: boolean;
  setLoading: (loading: boolean) => void;
}
```

---

## Live Demo

https://launcher.tunajam.com
